memo_surfaces.name: charter
memo_surfaces.summary: Role, mission, and hard rules of aoa-memo as the explicit memory and recall layer.
memo_surfaces.one_line_intent: Orient a reader to what aoa-memo is for, what it owns, and what it refuses to become.
memo_surfaces.use_when_short: Use when deciding whether a proposed addition belongs in aoa-memo or in a neighboring AoA or ToS repository.
memo_surfaces.do_not_use_short: Avoid when the need is execution logic, proof verdicts, routing logic, or runtime storage implementation.
memo_surfaces.inputs_short: Needs a question about role, ownership, mission, or non-goals.
memo_surfaces.outputs_short: Produces mission-level orientation, rule-level constraints, and success criteria for the memory layer.
memo_surfaces.core_contract_short: Core contract: memory stays explicit, bounded, and distinct from proof, routing, role policy, and source-authored knowledge.
memo_surfaces.main_risk_short: Main risk: charter language gets stretched into permission for aoa-memo to swallow neighboring layers.
memo_surfaces.validation_short: Validate by checking the proposed work still keeps memory distinct from proof, routing, role policy, and infra.

memo_surfaces.name: memory-model
memo_surfaces.summary: Conceptual model for functions, temperatures, scopes, trust posture, and KAG orientation.
memo_surfaces.one_line_intent: Give aoa-memo a multi-axis model of memory so the layer can age, relate, and route without collapsing into vague long-context fog.
memo_surfaces.use_when_short: Use when the task touches memory classes, temperatures, scopes, trust posture, or KAG readiness.
memo_surfaces.do_not_use_short: Avoid when the task is purely repo ownership, routing implementation, or runtime storage plumbing.
memo_surfaces.inputs_short: Needs a conceptual question about memory behavior or memory object interpretation.
memo_surfaces.outputs_short: Produces stable vocabulary for memory functions, temperatures, scopes, trust posture, and recall modes.
memo_surfaces.core_contract_short: Core contract: memory is temporal and provenance-aware, and temperature never stands in for truth or proof.
memo_surfaces.main_risk_short: Main risk: collapsing confidence, authority, freshness, and salience into a single pseudo-score.
memo_surfaces.validation_short: Validate by checking the proposed change preserves the four-axis model and keeps graph lifts downstream.

memo_surfaces.name: boundaries
memo_surfaces.summary: Ownership map for what belongs in aoa-memo and what should stay in neighboring AoA or ToS layers.
memo_surfaces.one_line_intent: Keep aoa-memo sharp by naming what it owns and what it must not quietly absorb.
memo_surfaces.use_when_short: Use when classifying proposed content, deciding ownership, or spotting layer drift.
memo_surfaces.do_not_use_short: Avoid when the problem is conceptual memory modeling rather than repository ownership.
memo_surfaces.inputs_short: Needs a proposed object, surface, or question that may cross repositories.
memo_surfaces.outputs_short: Produces ownership guidance, anti-pattern checks, and safe outward-link posture.
memo_surfaces.core_contract_short: Core contract: aoa-memo owns explicit memory-layer meaning and points outward instead of replacing source repos.
memo_surfaces.main_risk_short: Main risk: turning a memory registry into a hidden graph platform or policy engine.
memo_surfaces.validation_short: Validate by checking the addition preserves source-of-truth boundaries and safe outward links.

memo_surfaces.name: roadmap
memo_surfaces.summary: Milestones for turning aoa-memo from bootstrap doctrine into a routeable, provenance-aware memory layer.
memo_surfaces.one_line_intent: Translate the memory doctrine into a staged build path that stays small, explicit, and integrable.
memo_surfaces.use_when_short: Use when selecting the next PR slice or sequencing work across aoa-memo, aoa-routing, aoa-kag, and aoa-agents.
memo_surfaces.do_not_use_short: Avoid when the need is a present-tense object definition rather than milestone planning.
memo_surfaces.inputs_short: Needs a question about implementation order, missing surfaces, or integration stages.
memo_surfaces.outputs_short: Produces milestone order, exit criteria, and cross-repo dependency hints.
memo_surfaces.core_contract_short: Core contract: aoa-memo grows by doctrine, schemas, generated surfaces, and contracts before heavier runtime or graph work.
memo_surfaces.main_risk_short: Main risk: skipping compact public surfaces and jumping too early into infra or graph theatrics.
memo_surfaces.validation_short: Validate by checking the proposed work advances one named milestone without blurring the non-goals.

memo_surfaces.name: narrative-core-contract
memo_surfaces.summary: Contract for keeping authored/core memory distinct from derived memory surfaces and downstream substrate work.
memo_surfaces.one_line_intent: Keep authored/core memory explicit while allowing derived recall, lift, and substrate work to stay downstream.
memo_surfaces.use_when_short: Use when deciding whether a memory-facing surface belongs in authored/core memo or in derived recall, KAG, or runtime layers.
memo_surfaces.do_not_use_short: Avoid when the task is purely about roadmap sequencing or repo ownership without touching authored-versus-derived memory boundaries.
memo_surfaces.inputs_short: Needs a question about core memory scope, derived recall work, or handoff boundaries into downstream systems.
memo_surfaces.outputs_short: Produces a clear authored/core versus derived split, supporting-structure posture, and handoff rules for downstream consumers.
memo_surfaces.core_contract_short: Core contract: write the memory event once, keep authored/core surfaces inspectable, and derive downstream memory artifacts later.
memo_surfaces.main_risk_short: Main risk: letting derived memory or graph-ready substrate quietly replace reviewable authored/core memory.
memo_surfaces.validation_short: Validate by checking the change preserves source refs, provenance posture, temporal posture, and bounded derivation language.

memo_surfaces.name: runtime-writeback-seam
memo_surfaces.summary: Bounded contract for mapping runtime-local state, checkpoint exports, and reviewed distillation candidates into the current memo canon.
memo_surfaces.one_line_intent: Keep runtime-local scratchpad out of durable memo while making checkpoint export and reviewed writeback rules explicit.
memo_surfaces.use_when_short: Use when deciding whether a runtime artifact should stay operational-only, export as a state capsule, or become a reviewed memo candidate.
memo_surfaces.do_not_use_short: Avoid when the task is role-rights policy, scenario choreography, or runtime storage implementation.
memo_surfaces.inputs_short: Needs a question about runtime-to-memo mapping, checkpoint export, or durable writeback boundaries.
memo_surfaces.outputs_short: Produces explicit mapping rules from runtime surfaces into memo kinds plus the review boundary for distillation candidates.
memo_surfaces.core_contract_short: Core contract: scratchpad stays runtime-local; checkpoint export becomes state_capsule; durable claims, patterns, and bridges remain review-gated candidates.
memo_surfaces.main_risk_short: Main risk: turning aoa-memo into a live runtime store or silently promoting runtime artifacts into canon.
memo_surfaces.validation_short: Validate by checking the checkpoint-to-memory contract preserves the mapping and keeps review required for distillation candidates.
